title: state: connection_counter Relaxed ordering needs comment — safe but subtle
description: relay/src/state.rs:153-156: connection_counter.fetch_add uses Ordering::Relaxed. The seq is always read from inside DashMap entry operations (which use internal Mutex), providing the necessary acquire/release barrier. The Relaxed ordering is safe because seq visibility is guaranteed by DashMap's locking, not by the atomic ordering itself. This is non-obvious; add a comment explaining why Relaxed is sufficient here: the DashMap shard lock provides the memory ordering barrier needed for seq to be visible to other threads reading the same pub_id's entry.
